message:
fix(installer): handle ExecStart=- prefix, fix user-scope error path

Three fixes from fresh-eyes review:

1. ExecStart parsing now handles systemd's optional - (ignore-error)
   prefix: ExecStart=-/usr/local/bin/sbh is correctly parsed to
   /usr/local/bin/sbh instead of -/usr/local/bin/sbh.

2. User-scope service branch: if install fails, the service is now
   restarted with the old binary (instead of being left stopped) and
   finish_phase reports accurate "failed" message instead of falling
   through to "service binary synced".

3. Pre-commit hook: removed 2>/dev/null from cargo fmt --check so
   real errors (missing rustfmt, broken toolchain) are visible instead
   of silently swallowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,7 +13,7 @@ fi
 
 # Run cargo fmt --check. If it fails, the commit is blocked with
 # a helpful message showing exactly what needs formatting.
-if ! cargo fmt --check 2>/dev/null; then
+if ! cargo fmt --check; then
   printf '\n\033[1;31mPre-commit: cargo fmt --check failed.\033[0m\n'
   printf 'Run \033[1mcargo fmt\033[0m to fix, then re-stage and commit.\n\n'
   exit 1

@@ -735,15 +735,17 @@ sync_system_service() {
   # Check system-level service
   local sys_unit="/etc/systemd/system/sbh.service"
   if [[ -f "$sys_unit" ]]; then
-    service_binary=$(grep '^ExecStart=' "$sys_unit" | head -1 | sed 's/^ExecStart=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+    # Strip ExecStart= prefix and optional - (systemd ignore-error marker),
+    # then take the first whitespace-delimited token (the binary path).
+    service_binary=$(grep '^ExecStart=' "$sys_unit" | head -1 | sed 's/^ExecStart=-\{0,1\}//' | awk '{print $1}')
     unit_scope="system"
   fi
 
   # Fallback: check user-level service
   if [[ -z "$service_binary" ]]; then
     local user_unit="${HOME}/.config/systemd/user/sbh.service"
     if [[ -f "$user_unit" ]]; then
-      service_binary=$(grep '^ExecStart=' "$user_unit" | head -1 | sed 's/^ExecStart=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+      service_binary=$(grep '^ExecStart=' "$user_unit" | head -1 | sed 's/^ExecStart=-\{0,1\}//' | awk '{print $1}')
       unit_scope="user"
     fi
   fi
@@ -809,6 +811,11 @@ sync_system_service() {
       log_info "Updated ${service_binary}"
     else
       log_warn "Failed to update ${service_binary}"
+      if $was_active; then
+        systemctl --user start sbh.service 2>/dev/null || true
+      fi
+      finish_phase "failed to update user service binary"
+      return 0
     fi
 
     if $was_active; then
@@ -817,7 +824,7 @@ sync_system_service() {
     fi
   fi
 
-  finish_phase "service binary synced and daemon restarted"
+  finish_phase "service binary synced"
 }
 
 print_summary() {
